---
title: "herald cheatsheet"
description: >
  Single-page reference for the herald CDISC validation pipeline -- read,
  spec, stamp, dictionaries, catalog, validate, report.
output:
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
    toc: false
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{herald cheatsheet}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = FALSE, comment = "")
```

::: {.cheatsheet}

::: {.cs-hero}
::: {}
# herald cheatsheet {.unnumbered}

Pure-R CDISC conformance validation. Read submission datasets, attach
metadata, run rules, ship reviewer-ready reports -- without Java or SAS.
:::
::: {.cs-hero-meta}
SDTM-IG v2.0 &middot; ADaM-IG v5.0 &middot; Define-XML 2.1<br>
CDISC Library CORE &middot; NCI EVS CT &middot; MedDRA &middot; WHODrug
:::
:::

::: {.cs-stack}
::: {.cs-stack-cell style="--cs-accent: #0ea5e9"}
[L0]{.cs-stack-num}<br>[Read]{.cs-stack-name}<br>[read_xpt / read_json]{.cs-stack-fn}
:::
::: {.cs-stack-cell style="--cs-accent: #6366f1"}
[L1]{.cs-stack-num}<br>[Spec]{.cs-stack-name}<br>[as_herald_spec]{.cs-stack-fn}
:::
::: {.cs-stack-cell style="--cs-accent: #8b5cf6"}
[L2]{.cs-stack-num}<br>[Stamp]{.cs-stack-name}<br>[apply_spec]{.cs-stack-fn}
:::
::: {.cs-stack-cell style="--cs-accent: #ec4899"}
[L3]{.cs-stack-num}<br>[Dict]{.cs-stack-name}<br>[ct_provider]{.cs-stack-fn}
:::
::: {.cs-stack-cell style="--cs-accent: #f59e0b"}
[L4]{.cs-stack-num}<br>[Catalog]{.cs-stack-name}<br>[rule_catalog]{.cs-stack-fn}
:::
::: {.cs-stack-cell style="--cs-accent: #10b981"}
[L5]{.cs-stack-num}<br>[Validate]{.cs-stack-name}<br>[validate]{.cs-stack-fn}
:::
::: {.cs-stack-cell style="--cs-accent: #ef4444"}
[L6]{.cs-stack-num}<br>[Report]{.cs-stack-name}<br>[write_report_html]{.cs-stack-fn}
:::
:::

::: {.cs-section}
## L0 -- Read

[Pure-R ingest. No JVM, no SAS.]{.cs-section-sub}
:::

::: {.cs-grid}

::: {.cs-card .cs-l0}
[Transport]{.cs-tag}

### SAS XPT v5

[Round-trip XPT with labels, lengths, types preserved.]{.cs-lede}

```r
dm <- read_xpt("dm.xpt")
write_xpt(dm, "dm.xpt", label = "Demographics")
```

::: {.cs-note}
**Tip:** XPT label = 40 char max, variable label = 200; truncation throws
`herald_error_input`.
:::
:::

::: {.cs-card .cs-l0}
[Modern]{.cs-tag}

### Dataset-JSON v1.1

[CDISC's transport replacement -- preserves types and ISO 8601 dates.]{.cs-lede}

```r
ae <- read_json("ae.json")
write_json(ae, "ae.json",
  dataset_label = "Adverse Events",
  iso_dates     = TRUE
)
```

::: {.cs-note}
Carries `name`, `label`, `itemOID`, `standard` on every column.
:::
:::

::: {.cs-card .cs-l0}
[Analytics]{.cs-tag}

### Parquet

[Arrow-backed columnar storage. Best for ADaM at scale.]{.cs-lede}

```r
adsl <- read_parquet("adsl.parquet")
write_parquet(adsl, "adsl.parquet")

convert_dataset("dm.xpt", "dm.parquet")
```

::: {.cs-note}
Requires `arrow`; falls back to a clear `herald_error_runtime` if absent.
:::
:::

:::

::: {.cs-section}
## L1 -- Spec

[Build the metadata object that drives stamping and validation.]{.cs-section-sub}
:::

::: {.cs-grid}

::: {.cs-card .cs-l1}
[From Define-XML]{.cs-tag}

### Lift metadata

[Round-trip a Define-XML 2.1 document into a herald_spec.]{.cs-lede}

```r
def  <- read_define_xml("define.xml")
spec <- as_herald_spec(def)

is_herald_spec(spec) # TRUE
```

::: {.cs-note}
Carries codelists, methods, comments, value-level metadata, ARM display
sections.
:::
:::

::: {.cs-card .cs-l1}
[From scratch]{.cs-tag}

### Hand-build a spec

[Programmatic construction for tests, fixtures, sponsor extensions.]{.cs-lede}

```r
spec <- herald_spec(
  datasets  = list(
    DM = list(label = "Demographics",
              class = "SPECIAL PURPOSE")
  ),
  variables = list(
    DM = list(
      USUBJID = list(label = "Subject ID",
                     length = 30, type = "text"),
      AGE     = list(label = "Age", type = "integer")
    )
  )
)
```
:::

::: {.cs-card .cs-l1}
[Inspect]{.cs-tag}

### Walk a spec

[Print, summarise, validate against the schema.]{.cs-lede}

```r
print(spec)
summary(spec)
validate_spec(spec)   # define-xml structure rules

names(spec$datasets)
spec$variables$DM$AGE
```

::: {.cs-note}
`validate_spec()` runs only the `DEFINE-NNN` rule subset -- no datasets
needed.
:::
:::

:::

::: {.cs-section}
## L2 -- Stamp

[Project spec attributes onto data frames before validation.]{.cs-section-sub}
:::

::: {.cs-grid-2}

::: {.cs-card .cs-l2}
[Apply]{.cs-tag}

### Stamp a single dataset

```r
dm <- apply_spec(dm, spec)

attr(dm, "label")             # "Demographics"
attr(dm$AGE, "label")         # "Age"
attr(dm$AGE, "herald_length") # 8
class(dm)                     # data.frame + sdtm class
```

::: {.cs-note}
Idempotent. Re-stamping replaces -- never appends.
:::
:::

::: {.cs-card .cs-l2}
[Bulk]{.cs-tag}

### Stamp a whole study

```r
files <- list(DM = dm, AE = ae, EX = ex, VS = vs)
files <- Map(apply_spec, files, list(spec))

# or pass to validate() and let it stamp
validate(files = files, spec = spec)
```

::: {.cs-note}
Rules expecting `herald_length`, `herald_type`, dataset class will
fail-fast without stamping.
:::
:::

:::

::: {.cs-section}
## L3 -- Dictionaries

[Controlled terminology + external dictionary providers.]{.cs-section-sub}
:::

::: {.cs-grid}

::: {.cs-card .cs-l3}
[CDISC CT]{.cs-tag}

### Bundled SDTM / ADaM CT

```r
ct <- load_ct("sdtm", release = "2024-09-27")
ct_info(ct)
available_ct_releases("sdtm")

# refresh local NCI EVS cache
download_ct("sdtm")
```
:::

::: {.cs-card .cs-l3}
[External]{.cs-tag}

### MedDRA / WHODrug / SRS

```r
mh   <- meddra_provider(path = "meddra/")
who  <- whodrug_provider(path = "whodrug/")
srs  <- srs_provider(release = "latest")

mh$contains("LLT", "Headache")
who$lookup("ATC", "N02BE01")
```
:::

::: {.cs-card .cs-l3}
[Custom]{.cs-tag}

### Sponsor / ad-hoc

```r
my_codelist <- custom_provider(
  values = c("AE001", "AE002"),
  name   = "SPONSOR_AE_REASON"
)

register_dictionary("SPONSOR", my_codelist)
list_dictionaries()
unregister_dictionary("SPONSOR")
```

::: {.cs-note}
All providers share `contains()` + optional `lookup()` -- rules don't
care about source.
:::
:::

:::

::: {.cs-section}
## L4 -- Catalog

[Inspect and filter the compiled rule corpus.]{.cs-section-sub}
:::

::: {.cs-grid-2}

::: {.cs-card .cs-l4}
[Browse]{.cs-tag}

### The rule catalog

```r
cat <- rule_catalog()

# slice by standard
sdtm  <- cat[cat$standard == "SDTM-IG", ]
adam  <- cat[cat$standard == "ADaM-IG", ]
core  <- cat[cat$standard == "CORE",    ]

supported_standards()
```
:::

::: {.cs-card .cs-l4}
[Filter]{.cs-tag}

### Pick targeted rule sets

```r
demog_rules <- cat[
  cat$dataset == "DM" &
    cat$severity == "error",
  "rule_id"
]

validate(files = files, spec = spec,
         rules = demog_rules)
```

::: {.cs-note}
Rule IDs are stable -- safe to pin in study-level QC scripts.
:::
:::

:::

::: {.cs-grid-2}

::: {.cs-ref}
#### Rule ID prefixes {.unnumbered}

| prefix             | source                              |
|--------------------|-------------------------------------|
| `SDTMIG-CGNNNN`    | SDTM-IG v2.0 (CG0001 -- CG0666)     |
| `ADaM-N`           | ADaM-IG v5.0 (1 -- ~790)            |
| `DEFINE-NNN`       | Define-XML 2.1 (001 -- 225)         |
| `CORE-NNNNNN`      | SDTM Library API (000001 -- 001082) |
:::

::: {.cs-ref}
#### Severity levels {.unnumbered}

| level     | meaning                    |
|-----------|----------------------------|
| `error`   | Submission-blocking        |
| `warning` | Reviewer follow-up expected|
| `note`    | Informational, advisory    |
:::

:::

::: {.cs-section}
## L5 -- Validate

[Run the catalog. Returns a herald_result.]{.cs-section-sub}
:::

::: {.cs-grid-2}

::: {.cs-card .cs-l5}
[Run]{.cs-tag}

### End-to-end validate()

```r
res <- validate(
  files = list(DM = dm, AE = ae),
  spec  = spec,
  rules = c("SDTMIG-CG0001",
            "CORE-000123")
)

summary(res)
```

::: {.cs-note}
Pass a directory path or a named list of data frames. Spec is optional
but unlocks metadata-aware rules.
:::
:::

::: {.cs-card .cs-l5}
[Tune]{.cs-tag}

### Severity + scope filters

```r
res <- validate(
  files        = "data/",
  spec         = spec,
  standards    = c("SDTM-IG", "Define-XML"),
  severity_map = c("SDTMIG-CG0042" = "warning"),
  dictionaries = list(MEDDRA = mh)
)
```

::: {.cs-note}
Skipped cross-dataset refs surface in `res$skipped_refs` -- not as
silent passes.
:::
:::

:::

::: {.cs-grid-2}

::: {.cs-ref}
#### Operator kinds (R/ops-*.R) {.unnumbered}

| kind        | example use                       |
|-------------|-----------------------------------|
| `set`       | Codelist membership               |
| `compare`   | Cross-variable comparisons        |
| `existence` | Variable presence / NA checks     |
| `temporal`  | Date order, ISO 8601 parsing      |
| `cross`     | Cross-dataset joins               |
| `string`    | Pattern, regex, length            |
:::

::: {.cs-ref}
#### Error classes (always via herald_error()) {.unnumbered}

| class                        | when                          |
|------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| `herald_error_input`         | User argument problem         |
| `herald_error_runtime`       | Internal / dependency failure |
| `herald_error_file`          | I/O, missing or unreadable    |
| `herald_error_spec`          | Spec construction or shape    |
| `herald_error_rule`          | Rule definition problem       |
| `herald_error_validation`    | Engine run failure            |
:::

:::

::: {.cs-section}
## L6 -- Report

[Reviewer-ready artifacts. Self-contained, audit-friendly.]{.cs-section-sub}
:::

::: {.cs-grid}

::: {.cs-card .cs-l6}
[HTML]{.cs-tag}

### Self-contained QC report

```r
write_report_html(res, "qc.html")
```

::: {.cs-note}
Findings, rule counts, dataset metadata, skipped refs, run metadata --
one file, no assets.
:::
:::

::: {.cs-card .cs-l6}
[XLSX]{.cs-tag}

### Workbook for reviewers

```r
write_report_xlsx(res, "qc.xlsx")
```

::: {.cs-note}
One sheet per section; severity-coloured rows.
:::
:::

::: {.cs-card .cs-l6}
[JSON]{.cs-tag}

### Machine-readable artifact

```r
write_report_json(res, "qc.json")

# or dispatch from extension
report(res, "out/qc.html")
report(res, "out/qc.xlsx")
```

::: {.cs-note}
Stable schema -- safe for downstream dashboards, CI, ticket creation.
:::
:::

:::

::: {.cs-section}
## End-to-end recipe

[A full submission QC pass in one block.]{.cs-section-sub}
:::

::: {.cs-recipe}

```r
library(herald)

# L0 + L1 -- read datasets and lift Define-XML metadata
def   <- read_define_xml("submission/define.xml")
spec  <- as_herald_spec(def)

files <- list(
  DM = read_xpt("submission/dm.xpt"),
  AE = read_json("submission/ae.json"),
  EX = read_xpt("submission/ex.xpt")
)

# L3 -- attach external dictionaries
mh  <- meddra_provider(path = "dict/meddra/")

# L5 -- run targeted validation
res <- validate(
  files        = files,
  spec         = spec,
  standards    = c("SDTM-IG", "Define-XML"),
  dictionaries = list(MEDDRA = mh),
  severity_map = c("SDTMIG-CG0042" = "warning")
)

# L6 -- ship reviewer artifacts
write_report_html(res, "out/qc.html")
write_report_xlsx(res, "out/qc.xlsx")
write_report_json(res, "out/qc.json")
```

:::

::: {.cs-section}
## Tips & pitfalls

[Things experienced users wish they knew on day one.]{.cs-section-sub}
:::

::: {.cs-tips-wrap}

- **Stamp before validate.** Metadata-aware rules silently skip on
  un-stamped data. `apply_spec()` first.
- **Pin rule IDs in CI.** Catalog grows -- `rules = c(...)` keeps
  reviews reproducible across releases.
- **Use `severity_map`, not forks.** Demote sponsor-irrelevant rules
  without editing YAML.
- **Inspect `res$skipped_refs`.** Cross-dataset rules skip when refs
  missing -- not a pass.
- **Errors are typed.** `tryCatch(..., herald_error_input = ...)`
  beats string matching.
- **Define-XML alone is valid input.** Run `validate_spec()` against
  `define.xml` with no datasets.
- **Parquet for big ADaM.** XPT > 2 GB is risky; `convert_dataset()`
  moves formats while preserving labels.
- **Custom providers cost nothing.** Wrap any vector with
  `custom_provider()` and register it.

:::

:::
